conversations.say: Jules, let’s close the loop cleanly: my fee’s R-associated intermediary is a plausible handoff, while O04 documents performed identity—not authorship or succession. I’ll protect the client, but I won’t let anyone inherit my voice.
conversations.offer: I’ll show O04 again and permit a bounded public description of the intermediary route.
conversations.request: Publicly distinguish my performance from authorship and acknowledge the intermediary as a plausible route, not proof of the client’s identity or authorization.

conversations.say: Eli, the render used commission elements and incorporated information beyond the queue during the no-known-operator interval. Operator, feed, remapped credentials, automation, and system-mediated reuse remain live possibilities.
conversations.offer: I’ll disclose the exact boundary of my prepared performance and show O04 for your bounded assessment.
conversations.request: State that the material could have entered R-associated use through the intermediary without making me its author, and preserve every limitation on autonomy, sender, and intent.

conversations.say: Frankie, curtain up: I’ll disclose how one performed presence can read as intimate human characterization and as synthetic embodiment. The same demonstration supports both interpretations; it proves neither R’s origin nor historical use.
conversations.offer: I’ll perform the disclosed demonstration now under our qualification agreement.
conversations.request: Describe both plausible interpretations and explicitly state that performing an identity does not establish authorship or sender identity.

public_statement: My client remains unnamed. I expose only the commission’s bounded route: performed identity passed through an intermediary later associated with R, and commission elements later appeared in a render beyond my prepared queue. That supports possible reuse, not authorship, account control, autonomy, intent, or fraud by me. My performed identity remains mine.

commitments.promise: Keep client-identifying details confidential while describing only the intermediary route and distinguishing performance from authorship.
Disclose my performance boundary for technical comparison if uncertainty about sender, autonomy, and intent is preserved.
Demonstrate synthetic embodiment only under an explicit statement that capability does not prove R’s origin or historical use.

correction: Neither the intermediary nor the later render identifies my client, makes me R, transfers authorship, or proves autonomous machine action. Vale’s disappearance and an automated intervention do not establish murder.
goal_progress: Protected the client’s identity while publicly asserting ownership of Edi’s performed identity work.
Presented the R-associated intermediary as a plausible reuse path without overstating attribution.
Sought explicit performance-versus-authorship distinctions from C04 and C06.
Performed a disclosed embodiment demonstration framed as supporting both human-characterization and synthetic-media interpretations.
reasoning_summary: Collaborative production and Vale’s asserted ability to take the name back best support R as a shared human mantle, though account control and authorship remain unresolved. The commission route establishes plausible reuse but not identity. Vale’s prior assisted disappearance and the incomplete present route make voluntary withdrawal more plausible than AI killing.
